title: Nanobiocatalyst and nanobiocatalytic membrane
abstract: A nanobiocatalytic membrane for a filtration system is provided which includes a filtration membrane and a plurality of nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles associated with the membrane, each of the nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles including a core, a coating at least partially surrounding the core, and a plurality of nanobiocatalysts coupled to the coating. Each of the plurality of nanobiocatalysts includes an antibacterial nanoparticle comprising bismuth, and a quorum quenching agent coupled to the antibacterial nanoparticle. A nanobiocatalyst nanoparticle for use with a water purification system is also provided. A method of forming a nanobiocatalytic membrane for a filtration system and a method of using a nanobiocatalytic membrane in a filtration system are also provided.
claims: Claims 1. A nanobiocatalytic membrane for a filtration system, the nanobiocatalytic membrane comprising: a filtration membrane; a plurality of nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles associated with the membrane, each of the nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles comprising; a core; a coating at least partially surrounding the core; a plurality of nanobiocatalysts coupled to the coating, each of the plurality of nanobiocatalysts comprising: an antibacterial nanoparticle comprising bismuth; and a quorum quenching agent coupled to the antibacterial nanoparticle. 2. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1, wherein the membrane includes pores having an average pore size and the plurality of nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles has an average diameter less than the average pore size. 3. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1, wherein the core comprises a ferromagnetic material. 4. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1 , wherein the antibacterial nanoparticle comprises lipophilic bismuth dimercaptopropanol. 5. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1, wherein the quorum quenching agent is a quorum quenching enzyme. 6. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 5, wherein the quorum quenching enzyme is acylase or lactonase. 7. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1, wherein the quorum quenching agent is immobilized on the antibacterial nanoparticle. 8. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 7, wherein the quorum quenching agent is immobilized on the antibacterial nanoparticle via glutaraldehyde. 9. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 3, wherein the ferromagnetic core comprises magnetite. 10. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1 , wherein the filter membrane comprises nanobiocatalysts independently associated with the membrane surface. 11. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1 , wherein the coating comprises a mesoporous fumed metal oxide. 12. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of any of claims 1-11, wherein the nanobiocatalyst is secured to the core by a thiol interaction. 13. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1, wherein the membrane has a first membrane surface and a second membrane surface opposite the first membrane surface, and wherein the membrane is a porous membrane with a plurality of pores extending therethrough from the first membrane surface to the second membrane surface. 14. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 13, wherein the membrane is an ultrafiltration membrane. 15. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 13, wherein the membrane is made of polyvinylidene difluoride. 16. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 13, wherein the membrane is a nanofiltration membrane. 17. The nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 1 , wherein the membrane is a reverse osmosis membrane. 18. A filtration system comprising: a fluid passageway; the nanobiocatalytic membrane of claim 3 positioned within the fluid passageway; a magnetic device positioned downstream of the nanobiocatalytic membrane, the magnetic device configured to recover the at least one magnetic core and the plurality of nanobiocatalysts coupled to the magnetic core after they pass through the membrane. 19. A nanobiocatalyst nanoparticle for use with a water purification system, the nanobiocatalyst comprising: a core; an outer layer substantially surrounding the core; a plurality of antibacterial nanoparticles coupled to the outer layer, the antibacterial nanoparticles comprising bismuth; and a plurality of quorum quenching enzymes immobilized on at least one of the plurality of antibacterial nanoparticles. 20. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, in combination with a membrane, the membrane having a first membrane surface and a second membrane surface opposite the first membrane surface, wherein the nanobiocatalyst is sized and configured to move through the membrane, from the first membrane surface through to the second membrane surface. 21. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 20, in combination with the membrane, wherein the membrane is a porous membrane with a plurality of pores extending therethrough from the first membrane surface to the second membrane surface. 22. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the core is ferromagnetic. 23. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the outer layer is mesoporous silica. 24. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the outer layer is a fumed metal oxide. 25. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the antibacterial nanoparticle comprises a bismuth dithiol. 26. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 25, wherein the antibacterial nanoparticle is lipophilic bismuth dimercaptopropanol. 27. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the quorum quenching enzyme is acylase. 28. The nanobiocatalyst of claim 19, wherein the quorum quenching enzyme is lactonase. 29. A method of forming a nanobiocatalytic membrane for a filtration system, the method comprising: providing a core; precipitating silica on the surface of the core to form a coated nanoparticle comprising a mesoporous silica outer layer; forming antibacterial nanoparticles and attaching the antibacterial nanoparticles to the coated nanoparticle via the silica in the mesoporous silica outer layer; immobilizing quorum quenching enzymes on the antibacterial nanoparticles with a binding agent; and providing a membrane and associating the coated nanoparticle with the membrane. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the coated nanoparticle is attached to the membrane after the antibacterial nanoparticles and quorum quenching enzymes are attached to the coated nanoparticle. 31. The method of claim 29, wherein the coated nanoparticle is attached to the membrane before the antibacterial nanoparticles and quorum quenching enzymes are attached to the coated nanoparticle. 32. The method of claim 29, wherein the antibacterial nanoparticle comprises bismuth. 33. The method of claim 32, wherein the antibacterial nanoparticle comprises lipophilic bismuth dimercaptopropanol. 34. The method of claim 29, wherein the quorum quenching enzyme is acylase. 35. The method of claim 29, wherein the quorum quenching enzyme is lactonase. 36. The method of claim 29, wherein the binding agent used to attach the quorum quenching enzyme to the antibacterial nanoparticle is glutar aldehyde. 37. The method of any of claims 29-36, wherein the core is ferromagnetic. 38. A method of using a nanobiocatalytic membrane in a filtration system, the method comprising: providing a nanobiocatalytic membrane in a fluid passageway, wherein the nanobiocatalytic membrane comprises at least one nanobiocatalyst nanoparticle coupled to a membrane, wherein the at least one nanobiocatalyst nanoparticle includes a core, a coating, a plurality of bismuth nanoparticles, and a plurality of quorum quenching enzymes; flowing a fluid through the passageway and across the nanobiocatalytic membrane; solubilizing bismuth from the bismuth nanoparticles; and interfering with intercellular communication to reduce the production of biofilms. 39. The method of claim 38, wherein the core is magnetic and the nanobiocatalyst nanoparticles are magnetically recovered after passing through the membrane. 40. The method of claim 38, wherein the nanobiocatalytic membrane is a, cross-flow membrane. 41. The method of any one of claims 38-40, wherein the quorum quenching enzymes are associated with the bismuth nanoparticle. 42. The method of claim 38, wherein the fluid is water. 43. The method of claim 39, further comprising reloading the at least one magnetic nanobiocatalyst onto the membrane.
